title: Power BI - REST API, Deployment and .pbix Structure
description: REST API endpoints for import/refresh/credentials, authentication, .pbix file format internals, workspace concepts

workspace_concepts.hierarchy: workspace → dataset (semantic model) → report (visualizations) → dashboard (pinned tiles from reports)
workspace_concepts.workspace: A container for Power BI content. Also called 'group'. Has a groupId (UUID). 'My Workspace' is personal and uses /myorg/ paths without a groupId.
workspace_concepts.dataset: The semantic model — contains the data model, Power Query transformations, DAX measures, and data. Reports are built on top of a dataset.
workspace_concepts.report: A collection of pages with visualizations. Always references exactly one dataset. Multiple reports can use the same dataset.
workspace_concepts.dashboard: A single page of tiles pinned from reports. Read-only, no editing.
workspace_concepts.app: A published bundle of workspace content (reports, dashboards) shared with end users. Separate from the workspace itself.

workspace_roles.Admin: Full control: add/remove members, publish, delete
workspace_roles.Contributor: Can create/edit content but not manage workspace

service_principal_flow.description: Register an Azure AD application, grant Power BI permissions, add service principal to the target workspace.
steps: 1. Register app in Entra ID (Azure AD): App registrations > New registration
2. Create a client secret: App > Certificates & secrets > New client secret
3. In Power BI Admin Portal: Tenant settings > Developer settings > 'Allow service principals to use Power BI APIs' > Enable for specific security group
4. Add the service principal to the workspace: Workspace > Manage access > Add the app by name > assign Member or Admin role
5. Acquire token: POST to token endpoint with grant_type=client_credentials

required_permissions.Dataset.ReadWrite.All: Required for importing .pbix files and managing datasets
required_permissions.Report.ReadWrite.All: Required for creating and managing reports
required_permissions.Workspace.ReadWrite.All: Required for managing workspace membership and content

authentication.important_limitation: Service principals CANNOT authenticate to SharePoint Online for scheduled refresh of SharePoint-sourced datasets. The dataset's data source credentials must use an OAuth2 service account (a licensed Microsoft 365 user) in Power BI Service settings.

import_pbix.description: Upload a .pbix file to a workspace. Returns 202 Accepted immediately; poll the Get Import endpoint to check status.
nameConflict_options: Ignore (default — creates a new dataset with the same name)
import_pbix.note: File must be sent as multipart/form-data body. Max file size via this endpoint is 1 GB. For 1-10 GB files, use Create Temporary Upload Location first.

list_reports.description: List all reports in a workspace. Returns report id, name, datasetId, embedUrl.

list_datasets.description: List all datasets in a workspace. Returns dataset id, name, isRefreshable, configuredBy.

trigger_refresh.description: Trigger an on-demand refresh of the dataset.
trigger_refresh.response: 202 Accepted (empty body). Get refresh history to track completion.
trigger_refresh.note: Refresh counts against the daily limit: 8/day (Pro), 48/day (Premium).

get_refresh_history.description: Get recent refresh history. Returns array of refresh objects with status, startTime, endTime, serviceExceptionJson.

update_datasource_credentials.description: Update credentials for a specific datasource within a dataset. Required after importing a .pbix before the first refresh.

get_datasources.description: Get datasource details for a dataset, including datasourceId and connection details. Use this to find the datasourceId needed for credential updates.

update_parameters.description: Update Power Query parameter values in a published dataset. Useful for changing the SharePoint site URL without re-publishing.

bind_to_gateway.description: Bind a dataset to an on-premises data gateway. Not needed for SharePoint Online sources.

export_report_pdf.description: Export a report to PDF, PPTX, PNG, or XLSX

import_pbix_full_example.description: Complete Python example for importing a .pbix file via the REST API
import_pbix_full_example.python_code:
import requests
import time

def get_access_token(tenant_id, client_id, client_secret):
    url = f"https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token"
    data = {
        "grant_type": "client_credentials",
        "client_id": client_id,
        "client_secret": client_secret,
        "scope": "https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default"
    }
    resp = requests.post(url, data=data)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()["access_token"]


def import_pbix(workspace_id, report_name, pbix_path, token):
    """Upload .pbix file to Power BI workspace. Returns import ID."""
    url = (
        f"https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/imports"
        f"?datasetDisplayName={report_name}&nameConflict=Overwrite"
    )
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    with open(pbix_path, "rb") as f:
        files = {"file": (report_name + ".pbix", f, "application/octet-stream")}
        resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, files=files)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()["id"]


def wait_for_import(workspace_id, import_id, token, timeout_seconds=300):
    """Poll import status until Succeeded or Failed."""
    url = f"https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/imports/{import_id}"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    start = time.time()
    while time.time() - start < timeout_seconds:
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        state = resp.json().get("importState")
        if state == "Succeeded":
            return resp.json()
        if state == "Failed":
            raise RuntimeError(f"Import failed: {resp.json()}")
        time.sleep(5)
    raise TimeoutError("Import did not complete within timeout")


def trigger_refresh(workspace_id, dataset_id, token):
    """Trigger an on-demand dataset refresh."""
    url = f"https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/groups/{workspace_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}/refreshes"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json={})
    resp.raise_for_status()  # 202 Accepted
    return True


# Usage example:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    TOKEN = get_access_token(
        tenant_id="your-tenant-id",
        client_id="your-app-id",
        client_secret="your-client-secret"
    )
    WORKSPACE_ID = "your-workspace-guid"

    import_id = import_pbix(WORKSPACE_ID, "MonthlyReport", "./MonthlyReport.pbix", TOKEN)
    result = wait_for_import(WORKSPACE_ID, import_id, TOKEN)
    dataset_id = result["datasets"][0]["id"]
    print(f"Imported dataset ID: {dataset_id}")

    trigger_refresh(WORKSPACE_ID, dataset_id, TOKEN)
    print("Refresh triggered")

pbix_file_structure.description: A .pbix file is a ZIP archive. You can rename it to .zip and unzip to inspect the contents.

contents.description: Compressed binary containing the Tabular model (VertiPaq/ABF format). Contains all tables, relationships, DAX measures, column hierarchies. This is the core semantic model data.
JSON file describing all report pages, visuals, their positions, sizes, field mappings, format settings. This is the report layout definition.
Custom images and other static assets embedded in the report
JSON containing datasource connection strings (server, database, SharePoint URL, etc.). Contains the M expressions that connect to external data.
Row-Level Security (RLS) role definitions and table filters
MIME type manifest listing the content type for each part of the ZIP
Plain text file containing the Power BI Desktop version that created the file
Additional report settings in JSON/binary format

important_notes: The DataModel is a compressed binary (Analysis Services VertiPaq format) — not human-readable JSON
The Report/Layout is human-readable JSON but the schema is not publicly documented for direct editing
Direct programmatic creation of .pbix files from scratch is NOT supported via official APIs
Power BI Desktop is required to create .pbix files

pbip_format.description: Power BI Project (.pbip) is an open, text-based alternative to .pbix. Introduced in Power BI Desktop as a preview feature. Better for source control and programmatic editing.
pbip_format.enable: File > Options and settings > Options > Preview features > Power BI Project (.pbip) save option
pbip_format.folder_structure:
MyReport/
├── MyReport.pbip               -- pointer file that opens the project
├── .gitignore                  -- auto-generated, excludes cache files
├── MyReport.Report/
│   ├── definition.pbir         -- report item pointer (JSON)
│   ├── report.json             -- report layout (not publicly documented schema)
│   ├── mobileState.json        -- mobile layout
│   └── StaticResources/        -- embedded images
└── MyReport.SemanticModel/
    ├── definition.pbism        -- semantic model pointer (JSON)
    ├── model.bim               -- tabular model in BIM format (JSON, documented)
    └── definition/
        ├── tables/             -- one .tmdl file per table
        ├── relationships.tmdl  -- all relationship definitions
        └── expressions.tmdl   -- named M expressions (data sources)
pbip_format.tmdl_format: Tabular Model Definition Language (TMDL) is the text format for semantic model objects. Each table, relationship, measure, and M expression has a corresponding .tmdl file. These are editable with VS Code + the TMDL extension.
pbip_format.programmatic_editing: The Tabular Object Model (TOM) .NET library (Microsoft.AnalysisServices.Tabular NuGet package) can deserialise TMDL metadata, make programmatic modifications, and serialise back. This is the official way to programmatically create or modify Power BI semantic models.
limitations: Cannot programmatically convert .pbix <-> .pbip (must use File > Save As in Power BI Desktop)
report.json schema is not publicly documented — manual editing unsupported
Maximum path length 260 chars on Windows (keep folder paths short)

option_1_pbix_via_api.approach: Create the .pbix in Power BI Desktop, then upload via REST API Import endpoint
option_1_pbix_via_api.when_to_use: When you have a template .pbix that just needs data refreshed or parameters updated
option_1_pbix_via_api.automation_pattern:
1. Maintain a template .pbix in source control
2. Use REST API to import it (nameConflict=Overwrite)
3. Use Update Parameters API to set SharePoint URL or other connection params
4. Trigger refresh via API

option_2_xmla_endpoint.approach: Use XMLA endpoint with TMSL (Tabular Model Scripting Language) or TOM to create/modify datasets directly in Premium workspaces
option_2_xmla_endpoint.when_to_use: When you need to create or modify a dataset (tables, measures, relationships) programmatically without a .pbix file
option_2_xmla_endpoint.limitations: Premium/Premium Per User or Fabric capacity required. Creates the dataset only — report visuals must still be created in Power BI Desktop.
tools: Python with pyodbc or Analysis Services client libraries

option_3_push_datasets.approach: Use Push Datasets API to stream data into a Power BI dataset without a .pbix
option_3_push_datasets.when_to_use: Real-time streaming scenarios. Data is pushed row-by-row from external systems.
option_3_push_datasets.limitation: No complex M queries or Power Query transformations. Limited DAX. Reports built on push datasets have limited functionality.

option_4_fabric_rest_api.approach: Use Fabric REST API (api.fabric.microsoft.com) with PBIP item definitions to create reports and semantic models in Fabric-enabled workspaces

scheduled_refresh_via_api.description: Configure scheduled refresh programmatically using the PATCH datasets endpoint

update_refresh_schedule.notes: Times are in HH:MM format. Days array accepts: Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday.

refresh_frequency_limits.Pro: Up to 8 scheduled refreshes per day
refresh_frequency_limits.PPU_or_Premium: Up to 48 scheduled refreshes per day
refresh_frequency_limits.inactivity_pause: Scheduled refresh is automatically paused after 2 months of no user activity on reports/dashboards built on the dataset

list_workspaces.note: Returns workspaces the authenticated user/SP is a member of

embed_tokens.description: Generate embed tokens for embedding Power BI reports in custom applications

generate_embed_token.note: Embed token is short-lived (default 1 hour). Embed using Power BI JavaScript SDK in your web app.
